questions.subject: data structures
questions.questionText: Which data structure uses FIFO (First In First Out) principle?
questions.explanation: Queue follows FIFO where the first inserted element is removed first.

questions.subject: data structures
questions.questionText: Which data structure uses LIFO (Last In First Out)?
questions.explanation: Stack follows LIFO where the last inserted element is removed first.

questions.subject: data structures
questions.questionText: Which data structure is used for hierarchical data?
questions.explanation: Tree represents hierarchical relationships between elements.

questions.subject: data structures
questions.questionText: Which data structure allows direct access using index?
questions.explanation: Arrays allow accessing elements directly using index.

questions.subject: data structures
questions.questionText: Which data structure consists of nodes connected by pointers?
questions.explanation: Linked lists are made of nodes connected via pointers.

questions.subject: data structures
questions.questionText: What is the top element of a stack called?
questions.explanation: The accessible end of a stack is called the top.

questions.subject: data structures
questions.questionText: Which operation adds an element to a stack?
questions.explanation: Push operation inserts an element into a stack.

questions.subject: data structures
questions.questionText: Which operation removes an element from a queue?
questions.explanation: Dequeue removes an element from the front of a queue.

questions.subject: data structures
questions.questionText: Which data structure is best for implementing recursion?
questions.explanation: Recursion uses the call stack to store function calls.

questions.subject: data structures
questions.questionText: Which data structure is used to represent graphs?
questions.explanation: Graphs are commonly represented using adjacency matrix or adjacency list.
